title: ugni molinae
intro: Ugni molinae, also known as Chilean guava or strawberry myrtle, is a flowering evergreen shrub native to Chile. The plant is valued for its medicinal properties, including its high content of antioxidants, flavonoids, and phenolic acids, which have been shown to have anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial effects. The health benefits of Ugni molinae include its ability to support immune function, reduce inflammation, and exhibit antioxidant activity, potentially protecting against cell damage and chronic diseases. The plant is traditionally used in herbal preparations, such as teas, infusions, and extracts, which are often used to treat digestive issues, respiratory problems, and skin conditions.
